articles.title: Entanglement Builds Space-Time. Now "Magic" Gives It Gravity
articles.summary: Entanglement between particles gives space-time its structure, building an environment where matter can move and satisfy the conditions of Einstein's theory of gravity. This breakthrough is based on the concept of "magic" or quantumness, which allows physicists to describe a black hole in a new way by imagining space-time as a collection of purely quantum particles. The discovery has shed light on how Wheeler's first statement about space acting on matter and back on space to curve was fulfilled, but the origin of his second statement remained mysterious until now.

articles.title: Tracing a powerful GNSS interference source over Europe
articles.summary: This paper analyzes a powerful space-based Global Navigation Satellite System interference source that has been causing widespread transient wide-area interference events over continental Europe, Greenland, and Canada since 2019. The source is identified as a constellation of Russian early warning satellites in Molniya orbits based on data collected from terrestrial GNSS reference stations. The authors develop a received-power-based detection framework to identify the source and apply identification techniques that blend received-power and time-difference-of-arrival measurements.

articles.title: Code is Cheap(er)
articles.summary: Code is Cheap(er) Carson Gross June 04, 2026 Code has gotten much cheaper to create in the last year thanks to AI tools that can generate reasonably decent code quickly. This means understanding code has become more expensive as it now requires reading and analyzing generated code rather than writing from scratch. As a result, developers need to still understand the underlying code, even if it's generated by an LLM, to avoid potential dangers such as producing ununderstandable code or introducing new semantics into a code base.

articles.title: When su replaced login for becoming another Unix login
articles.summary: A recent change to the Linux kernel has allowed users to log in using their SSH username instead of their traditional Unix-style login credentials. This shift is significant because it enables developers to more easily switch between different operating systems and environments, as they can use a single set of credentials across multiple platforms. The new feature also facilitates seamless integration with other tools and services that support SSH authentication.

articles.title: IPv6 zones in URLs are a mistake
articles.summary: IPv6 zones in URLs are a mistake because they can cause confusion when trying to disambiguate host and port numbers, especially with URL encoding. This is due to the way IPv6 addresses are represented as part of the hostname, which can lead to errors when parsing URLs. To work around this issue, percent-encoding the percent sign in the IPv6 zone is necessary, but there's no guidance on how to properly handle it for URLs in RFC 9884 and other frameworks.

articles.title: Reviewing code requires reading
articles.summary: Reviewing code requires reading to diffuse responsibility and make it team effort, removing the burden from individual writers. This process also helps in learning the codebase by forcing people to have a look into different parts of the system, reducing bus factor and increasing familiarity with code culture.
